free_text: Gamma decay of 39K levels measured with the
38Ar(p,g)39K reaction.
Argon gas, enriched to 25% was used to produce
isotopically pure targets of 38Ar. The ions had an
energy of 45 keV. The target thickness turned out to
be 4.5 keV for 1.4 MeV protons. Argon targets in
tantalum produced at 45 keV ion energy have a
saturation value of 3.7 mu-g/cm2 of argon gas.
Data digitizing error
COMMON and REACTION records in Sub 002
were changed.
Data are given in counts per 20 mu-Coulomb.
10*10 cm NaI crystal was used.
Energy digitizing error
Gamma rays of the energy 2.0-9.0 MeV were detected.
Fig. 1
A 50 cm2 Ge(Li) detector at a distance of 1.5 cm
from target to front of crystal was used.
